title: minuartia graminifolia subsp. clandestina
intro: Minuartia graminifolia subsp. clandestina, also known as Alpine Clover, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, due to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and other bioactive compounds. Alpine Clover can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine.
